subject: DevOps
title: Facebook's Six-Hour Outage (2021)
description: A micro-lesson that makes Facebook's Six-Hour Outage (2021) usable.

hook.text: October 4, 2021. Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp vanished. All of them. For six hours.

buildup.text: A routine BGP config change withdrew Facebook's routes. The internet forgot how to find them.

discovery.text: Their DNS servers became unreachable. No DNS meant no login to internal tools to fix it.

twist.text: Engineers couldn't badge into data centers either. The badge system depended on the network.

climax.text: They had to physically access routers to restore BGP. Six hours of global silence followed.

punchline.text: Don't depend on yourself to fix yourself. Keep out-of-band access.

quiz.question: Why couldn't Facebook engineers quickly fix the 2021 outage?
options: Internal tools depended on the same network that was down
The code was too complex to understand
